title: Solar Panels vs. Grid Power
description: Compare the long-term costs, environmental impact, and reliability of solar panels versus traditional grid electricity.
verdict: Solar panels win for long-term savings and energy independence, despite higher upfront costs.

faqs.question: How much do solar panels save compared to grid power?
faqs.answer: Most homeowners save $20,000-$50,000 over 20 years by switching to solar, depending on local electricity rates and sun exposure.

faqs.question: Is grid power more reliable than solar?
faqs.answer: Grid power has historically been more reliable, but with battery storage, solar systems can provide backup power during outages.

faqs.question: Can solar panels fully replace grid electricity?
faqs.answer: With sufficient panel capacity and battery storage, many homes can offset 80-100% of their grid electricity usage.

title: Solar Loan vs. Solar Lease
description: Compare solar loans and solar leases to find the best financing option for your home solar installation.
verdict: Solar loans offer greater savings and ownership benefits, while leases provide simplicity with lower risk.

faqs.question: Which is better: solar loan or solar lease?
faqs.answer: Solar loans are better for maximizing savings since you own the system and claim the federal tax credit. Leases are better if you want zero maintenance responsibility.

faqs.question: Can I sell my house with a solar lease?
faqs.answer: Yes, but the lease must be transferred to the new buyer, which can complicate the sale process.

faqs.question: Do solar loans require good credit?
faqs.answer: Most solar loans require a credit score of 600-680+, though some lenders offer options for lower scores at higher interest rates.

title: Monocrystalline vs. Polycrystalline Solar Panels
description: Compare monocrystalline and polycrystalline solar panels to determine which type is best for your home.
verdict: Monocrystalline panels are the better choice for most homeowners due to higher efficiency and better aesthetics.

faqs.question: Are monocrystalline panels worth the extra cost?
faqs.answer: Yes, for most installations. Higher efficiency means fewer panels needed, which can offset the higher per-panel cost.

faqs.question: Which solar panel type lasts longer?
faqs.answer: Both types last 25+ years, but monocrystalline panels tend to maintain higher output over time.

faqs.question: What panel type do most installers recommend?
faqs.answer: Most installers recommend monocrystalline panels for residential use due to their superior efficiency and compact size.

title: Solar Battery Storage vs. Grid-Tied Only
description: Compare solar systems with battery storage versus grid-tied only systems to decide if a battery is worth the investment.
verdict: Battery storage adds resilience and energy independence but at a higher cost. Best for areas with frequent outages or TOU rates.

faqs.question: Is a solar battery worth the extra cost?
faqs.answer: If you experience frequent power outages or have time-of-use electricity rates, a battery can provide significant value and peace of mind.

faqs.question: How long does a solar battery last?
faqs.answer: Most solar batteries last 10-15 years with warranties typically covering 10 years or a certain number of charge cycles.

faqs.question: Can I add a battery to my existing solar system?
faqs.answer: Yes, most solar systems can be retrofitted with a battery, though costs may be higher than installing both simultaneously.

title: SunPower vs. Tesla Solar Panels
description: Compare SunPower and Tesla solar panels on efficiency, cost, warranties, and overall value for homeowners.
verdict: SunPower offers premium efficiency and warranties, while Tesla provides competitive pricing with seamless battery integration.

faqs.question: Are SunPower panels better than Tesla?
faqs.answer: SunPower panels are more efficient and have a stronger warranty, but Tesla panels cost less and integrate seamlessly with the Powerwall battery.

faqs.question: Which solar company has better customer service?
faqs.answer: SunPower generally receives higher customer satisfaction ratings, though Tesla has been improving its service network.

faqs.question: Can I mix SunPower panels with Tesla Powerwall?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tesla Powerwall is compatible with most solar panel brands, including SunPower.

title: Net Metering vs. Time-of-Use Rates
description: Compare net metering and time-of-use rate structures to understand how they affect your solar savings.
verdict: Net metering offers simpler savings, but TOU rates combined with battery storage can maximize returns in many markets.

faqs.question: Is net metering going away?
faqs.answer: Several states are transitioning away from full retail net metering to alternative compensation structures, but the timeline varies significantly.

faqs.question: How do TOU rates affect solar savings?
faqs.answer: TOU rates mean your solar generation is worth more during peak hours and less during off-peak, making battery storage more valuable.

faqs.question: Which is better for solar: net metering or TOU?
faqs.answer: Full retail net metering typically provides better solar savings, but TOU with battery storage can outperform in markets where net metering credits are reduced.

title: Cash Purchase vs. Solar Financing
description: Compare paying cash for solar panels versus using financing options to determine the best approach for your budget.
verdict: Cash purchases maximize total savings, but financing makes solar accessible to more homeowners with strong returns.

faqs.question: Is it better to pay cash for solar panels?
faqs.answer: Paying cash maximizes your total savings by eliminating interest costs, but financing lets you go solar with little to no money down.

faqs.question: What is the average ROI for a cash solar purchase?
faqs.answer: Cash buyers typically see a 300-500% return on investment over 20 years, depending on local electricity rates.

faqs.question: Do I still get the tax credit if I finance?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 30% federal solar tax credit applies whether you pay cash, use a loan, or use PACE financing. Leases and PPAs do not qualify.

title: Roof-Mounted vs. Ground-Mounted Solar
description: Compare roof-mounted and ground-mounted solar installations to find the best option for your property.
verdict: Roof-mounted is more cost-effective for most homeowners, while ground-mounted offers better performance for larger properties.

faqs.question: Are ground-mounted solar panels more efficient?
faqs.answer: Ground-mounted panels can be angled and oriented optimally, potentially producing 10-20% more energy than roof-mounted systems.

faqs.question: How much more do ground-mounted solar panels cost?
faqs.answer: Ground-mounted systems typically cost 20-30% more than roof-mounted due to additional racking and foundation requirements.

faqs.question: Do I need a lot of land for ground-mounted solar?
faqs.answer: A typical residential system needs about 400-600 square feet of ground space, roughly the size of a two-car garage.
